title: Observation rescues bare Exception instead of StandardError
body: Observation::RESCUES is set to [Exception], which means an experiment's candidate block can swallow things like SystemExit or NoMemoryError from a candidate path and just record them as an observation exception instead of propagating. The comment even says this will change to [StandardError] in the next major release -- could we get a migration path or opt-in flag sooner?

title: MismatchError#to_s crashes when candidate observation has a nil backtrace
body: MismatchError#format_observation calls observation.exception.backtrace.map { ... } assuming backtrace is always an array, but exceptions raised and immediately re-raised without ever propagating up a call stack (or created via Exception.new without raise) can have a nil backtrace, causing a NoMethodError while trying to render the mismatch error itself.

title: raise_on_mismatches class attribute isn't thread-safe for parallel test suites
body: RaiseOnMismatch#raise_on_mismatches= sets @raise_on_mismatches as a class-level ivar on the experiment class. When specs run in parallel (e.g. via parallel_tests) and toggle raise_on_mismatches on the same experiment class from different threads, one suite's setting can leak into another's assertions.

title: Document how fabricated_duration Hash keys interact with cpu_time default
body: Observation#initialize accepts fabricated_duration as a Hash with 'duration'/'cpu_time' keys or as a bare number (where cpu_time defaults to 0.0), but this dual behavior isn't documented anywhere outside the source, which makes it easy to misuse in custom test doubles.

title: Add ability to compare candidate results with a custom equality that ignores ordering
body: Observation#equivalent_to? supports a custom comparator proc but there's no built-in helper for the common case of comparing two Arrays/Hashes while ignoring element order, which every team seems to reimplement in their experiments' compare block.
